question_text: The number of minutes spent by two students, X and Y, exercising every day in a given week are shown in the bar chart above. The number of days in the given week in which one of the students spent a minimum of 10% more than the other student, on a given day, is
solution:
From the bar chart, we compare the minutes spent by students X and Y on each day. We
need to find the days where one student spent at least 10% more time than the other. For each
day, we calculate the percentage difference using the formula:
Percentage Difference =
Minutes of X −Minutes of Y
Minutes of Y
     × 100
- For Monday: |70 −45|/45 × 100 = 55.56% (X spent more)
- For Tuesday: |60 −55|/55 × 100 = 9.09% (No 10% difference)
- For Wednesday: |65 −60|/60 × 100 = 8.33% (No 10% difference)
- For Thursday: |60 −55|/55 × 100 = 9.09% (No 10% difference)
- For Friday: |50 −35|/35 × 100 = 42.86% (X spent more)
- For Saturday: |55 −50|/50 × 100 = 10% (No 10% difference)
- For Sunday: |65 −55|/55 × 100 = 18.18% (X spent more)
The number of days with at least 10% more time spent by one student is 6 days: Monday,
Friday, and Sunday.
Thus, the correct answer is (C) 6.
Final Answer: (C) 6
Quick Tip
To find the percentage difference in time spent, use the formula
  Time of X−Time of Y
Time of Y
  ×100
and check if it exceeds 10%.
